introSection: Taipan was a travelling steel roller coaster located in Australia.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The roller coaster opened in 1981 at Sea World and was called Wild Wave Rollercoaster. It was later renamed Thrillseeker. The ride was closed in May 2002. After it left Sea World, the roller coaster was repainted and named Taipan. It subsequently travelled with Shorts Amusements.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Yellow track and white supports. The ride used to feature blue track and white supports having white track and blue supports before that.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: A taipan is a type of snake in Australia. It is considered to be one of the most deadly snakes.

fullText:
Taipan was a travelling steel roller coaster located in Australia.

The roller coaster opened in 1981 at Sea World and was called Wild Wave Rollercoaster. It was later renamed Thrillseeker. The ride was closed in May 2002. After it left Sea World, the roller coaster was repainted and named Taipan. It subsequently travelled with Shorts Amusements.



Yellow track and white supports. The ride used to feature blue track and white supports having white track and blue supports before that.



A taipan is a type of snake in Australia. It is considered to be one of the most deadly snakes.
